findings: Negative class converged at 14/15 (~93%) across all iterations — the description's DO-NOT guidance is consistently respected.
Positive class is structurally bounded at ~13-27% under Copilot CLI regardless of description framing (imperative, lean verb-list, or helpfulness reframing).

Many failing positive prompts reference specific file paths (e.g. 'src/auth/middleware.ts', 'the dark mode toggle') that don't exist in the taskbean repo. Copilot grounds in the actual repo, finds the file missing, and asks for clarification instead of starting work — so it never reaches the step that would call `bean add`. This is an eval methodology issue more than a skill description issue.
Future work: rewrite positive prompts to be self-contained (no specific file paths) OR run evals from a fixture project where referenced files exist.
